response: Longtime NPR host David Greene sues Google over the male voice in its NotebookLM tool, which he claims is based on his voice. Greene alleges that after noticing similarities to his own cadence and filler words, he became convinced the voice was a copy of him. This isn't the first time an AI-generated voice has led to legal disputes; OpenAI removed a ChatGPT voice when actress Scarlett Johansson complained.
